title: campanula persicifolia subvar. pumila
intro: Campanula persicifolia subvar. pumila, also known as dwarf bellflower, is a variety of the Campanula persicifolia plant, characterized by its compact growth habit and bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula persicifolia subvar. pumila include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Campanula persicifolia subvar. pumila can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
